terms.definition: Algorithmic pattern that uses two indices over a sequence to solve pair/partition problems in O(n).
Algorithmic pattern that maintains a contiguous range `[l, r)` over a sequence and slides one endpoint at a time.
Two-pointer variant where both pointers start at the beginning and move at different speeds; canonical use is cycle detection in linked lists.
Range `[l, r)` that includes `l` but excludes `r`; the standard convention for window bounds to avoid off-by-one bugs.
A `collections.deque` kept monotonically increasing/decreasing; used to support O(1) rolling max/min over a sliding window.
Sliding-window flavor where the window length is the answer; expanded and shrunk based on a monotonic validity predicate.
Sliding-window flavor where the length k is given; updated with constant-time add/remove.
